methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $13M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Sarla Aviation is a Series A stage transportation company that has raised $13M to date. The broader transportation sector includes 8 private comparables, among them REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger; no public peers were identified with comparable profiles.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement to the $13M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $26M. A Peer-Median approach was not viable; while 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier comparables returned fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Using an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity.

The triangulation, anchored to a single method, supports a valuation range of $22M to $30M, with a median of $26M. This range reflects reasonable sensitivity around the stage-progression multiplier (±15% variance). Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable peer valuations at Sarla's stage and sector, the reliance on a single quantitative method, and limited visibility into company-specific metrics beyond capital raised. Further diligence on burn rate, revenue traction, and market positioning would be required to refine this estimate.
